segments.title: Live question system setup
segments.summary: The stream explains how audience questions should be submitted so they can be tracked rather than lost in chat.
segments.body: This chapter is useful archive structure evidence: live-stream pages need Q&A segments that can preserve the prompt, timestamp, and source context separately from ordinary topical notes.

evidence.note: The transcript discusses adding questions to the question system.

segments.title: Modern Navy equivalent of the Blackburn Blackburn
segments.question: What is the modern Navy equivalent of the Blackburn Blackburn for this channel?
segments.answerShort: The prototype keeps this as a timestamped Q&A seed for later transcript-backed answer cleanup.
segments.summary: A live Q&A prompt asks for a modern Navy analogue to the Blackburn Blackburn, giving the prototype a real `qa` segment to render and search.
segments.body: This segment deliberately records the question and source window without over-polishing the answer. It proves that Q&A can remain a segment subtype while still carrying question-specific fields.

evidence.note: The transcript contains the modern Navy equivalent question and setup.

segments.title: Spanish 1950s destroyer escorts
segments.summary: A discussion of Spanish Navy destroyer escorts frames them as useful ships, but as gun-and-torpedo designs in a naval world moving beyond that model.
segments.body: The segment shows how ordinary subject notes should work: it is not a fabricated Q&A entry, but a topical point with timestamped transcript evidence and tags for search and browsing.

evidence.note: The transcript discusses Spanish Navy destroyer escorts and gun-and-torpedo destroyer design.

segments.title: Carrier group force structure sketch
segments.summary: A force-structure answer sketches a carrier group built around carriers, LHDs, air-defense destroyers, ASW frigates, and submarines.
segments.body: This segment tests multi-topic browsing and search metadata: the same timestamp belongs under carrier groups, naval aviation, destroyers, and modern navy planning.

evidence.note: The transcript lists carriers, LHDs, air-defense destroyers, ASW frigates, and SSNs around a carrier group.
